verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचएवमेतत्पुरा पृष्टो मैत्रेयो भगवान् किल ।क्षत्‍त्रावनं प्रविष्टेन त्यक्त्वा स्वगृहमृद्धिमत् ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaevam etat purā pṛṣṭomaitreyo bhagavān kilakṣattrā vanaṁ praviṣṭenatyaktvā sva-gṛham ṛddhimat
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: After renouncing his prosperous home and entering the forest, King Vidura, the great devotee, asked this question of His Grace Maitreya Ṛṣi.

verses.sanskrit: यद्वा अयं मन्त्रकृद्वो भगवानखिलेश्वर: ।पौरवेन्द्रगृहंहित्वा प्रविवेशात्मसात्कृतम् ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: yad vā ayaṁ mantra-kṛd vobhagavān akhileśvaraḥpauravendra-gṛhaṁ hitvāpraviveśātmasāt kṛtam
verses.text: What else is there to say about the residential house of the Pāṇḍavas? Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of everything, acted as your minister. He used to enter that house as if it were His own, and He did not take any care of Duryodhana’s house.

verses.sanskrit: राजोवाचकुत्र क्षत्तुर्भगवता मैत्रेयेणास सङ्गम: ।कदावा सह संवाद एतद्वर्णय न: प्रभो ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: rājovācakutra kṣattur bhagavatāmaitreyeṇāsa saṅgamaḥkadā vā saha-saṁvādaetad varṇaya naḥ prabho
verses.text: The King asked Śukadeva Gosvāmī: Where and when did the meeting and discussion take place between Saint Vidura and His Grace Maitreya Muni? Kindly oblige, my lord, and describe this to us.

verses.sanskrit: न ह्यल्पार्थोदयस्तस्य विदुरस्यामलात्मन: ।तस्मिन् वरीयसि प्रश्न: साधुवादोपबृंहित: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: na hy alpārthodayas tasyavidurasyāmalātmanaḥtasmin varīyasi praśnaḥsādhu-vādopabṛṁhitaḥ
verses.text: Saint Vidura was a great and pure devotee of the Lord, and therefore his questions to His Grace Ṛṣi Maitreya must have been very purposeful, on the highest level, and approved by learned circles.

verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचस एवमृषिवर्योऽयं पृष्टो राज्ञा परीक्षिता ।प्रत्याह तं सुबहुवित्प्रीतात्मा श्रूयतामिति ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācasa evam ṛṣi-varyo ’yaṁpṛṣṭo rājñā parīkṣitāpratyāha taṁ subahu-vitprītātmā śrūyatām iti
verses.text: Śrī Suta Gosvāmī said: The great sage Śukadeva Gosvāmī was highly experienced and was pleased with the King. Thus being questioned by the King, he said to him, “Please hear the topics attentively.”

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचयदा तु राजा स्वसुतानसाधून्पुष्णन्नधर्मेण विनष्टद‍ृष्टि: ।भ्रातुर्यविष्ठस्य सुतान् विबन्धून्प्रवेश्य लाक्षाभवने ददाह ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācayadā tu rājā sva-sutān asādhūnpuṣṇan na dharmeṇa vinaṣṭa-dṛṣṭiḥbhrātur yaviṣṭhasya sutān vibandhūnpraveśya lākṣā-bhavane dadāha
verses.text: Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: King Dhṛtarāṣṭra became blind under the influence of impious desires to nourish his dishonest sons, and thus he set fire to the lacquer house to burn his fatherless nephews, the Pāṇḍavas.

verses.sanskrit: यदा सभायां कुरुदेवदेव्या:केशाभिमर्शं सुतकर्म गर्ह्यम् ।न वारयामास नृप: स्‍नुषाया:स्वास्रैर्हरन्त्या: कुचकुङ्कुमानि ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: yadā sabhāyāṁ kuru-deva-devyāḥkeśābhimarśaṁ suta-karma garhyamna vārayām āsa nṛpaḥ snuṣāyāḥsvāsrair harantyāḥ kuca-kuṅkumāni
verses.text: The King did not forbid his son Duḥśāsana’s abominable action of grabbing the hair of Draupadī, the wife of the godly King Yudhiṣṭhira, even though her tears washed the red dust on her breast.

verses.sanskrit: द्यूते त्वधर्मेण जितस्य साधो:सत्यावलम्बस्य वनं गतस्य ।न याचतोऽदात्समयेन दायंतमोजुषाणो यदजातशत्रो: ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: dyūte tv adharmeṇa jitasya sādhoḥsatyāvalambasya vanaṁ gatasyana yācato ’dāt samayena dāyaṁtamo-juṣāṇo yad ajāta-śatroḥ
verses.text: Yudhiṣṭhira, who was born without any enemy, was unfairly defeated in gambling. But because he had taken the vow of truthfulness, he went off to the forest. When he came back in due course and begged the return of his rightful share of the kingdom, he was refused by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who was overwhelmed by illusion.

verses.sanskrit: यदा च पार्थप्रहित: सभायांजगद्गुरुर्यानि जगाद कृष्ण: ।न तानि पुंसाममृतायनानिराजोरु मेने क्षतपुण्यलेश: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: yadā ca pārtha-prahitaḥ sabhāyāṁjagad-gurur yāni jagāda kṛṣṇaḥna tāni puṁsām amṛtāyanānirājoru mene kṣata-puṇya-leśaḥ
verses.text: Lord Kṛṣṇa was sent by Arjuna into the assembly as the spiritual master of the whole world, and although His words were heard by some [like Bhīṣma] as pure nectar, it was not so for the others, who were completely bereft of the last farthing of past pious works. The King [Dhṛtarāṣṭra or Duryodhana] did not take the words of Lord Kṛṣṇa very seriously.

verses.sanskrit: यदोपहूतो भवनं प्रविष्टोमन्त्राय पृष्ट: किल पूर्वजेन ।अथाह तन्मन्त्रद‍ृशां वरीयान्यन्मन्त्रिणो वैदुरिकं वदन्ति ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: yadopahūto bhavanaṁ praviṣṭomantrāya pṛṣṭaḥ kila pūrvajenaathāha tan mantra-dṛśāṁ varīyānyan mantriṇo vaidurikaṁ vadanti
verses.text: When Vidura was invited by his elder brother [Dhṛtarāṣṭra] for consultation, he entered the house and gave instructions which were exactly to the point. His advice is well known, and instructions by Vidura are approved by expert ministers of state.

verses.sanskrit: अजातशत्रो: प्रतियच्छ दायंतितिक्षतो दुर्विषहं तवाग: ।सहानुजो यत्र वृकोदराहि:श्वसन् रुषा यत्त्वमलं बिभेषि ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: ajāta-śatroḥ pratiyaccha dāyaṁtitikṣato durviṣahaṁ tavāgaḥsahānujo yatra vṛkodarāhiḥśvasan ruṣā yat tvam alaṁ bibheṣi
verses.text: [Vidura said:] You must now return the legitimate share to Yudhiṣṭhira, who has no enemies and who has been forbearing through untold sufferings due to your offenses. He is waiting with his younger brothers, among whom is the revengeful Bhīma, breathing heavily like a snake. Surely you are afraid of him.

verses.sanskrit: पार्थांस्तु देवो भगवान्मुकुन्दोगृहीतवान् सक्षितिदेवदेव: ।आस्ते स्वपुर्यां यदुदेवदेवोविनिर्जिताशेषनृदेवदेव: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: pārthāṁs tu devo bhagavān mukundogṛhītavān sakṣiti-deva-devaḥāste sva-puryāṁ yadu-deva-devovinirjitāśeṣa-nṛdeva-devaḥ
verses.text: Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Personality of Godhead, has accepted the sons of Pṛthā as His kinsmen, and all the kings of the world are with Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa. He is present in His home with all His family members, the kings and princes of the Yadu dynasty, who have conquered an unlimited number of rulers, and He is their Lord.

verses.sanskrit: स एष दोष: पुरुषद्विडास्तेगृहान् प्रविष्टो यमपत्यमत्या ।पुष्णासि कृष्णाद्विमुखो गतश्री-स्त्यजाश्वशैवं कुलकौशलाय ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa eṣa doṣaḥ puruṣa-dviḍ āstegṛhān praviṣṭo yam apatya-matyāpuṣṇāsi kṛṣṇād vimukho gata-śrīstyajāśv aśaivaṁ kula-kauśalāya
verses.text: You are maintaining offense personified, Duryodhana, as your infallible son, but he is envious of Lord Kṛṣṇa. And because you are thus maintaining a nondevotee of Kṛṣṇa, you are devoid of all auspicious qualities. Relieve yourself of this ill fortune as soon as possible and do good to the whole family!

verses.sanskrit: इत्यूचिवांस्तत्र सुयोधनेनप्रवृद्धकोपस्फुरिताधरेण ।असत्कृत: सत्स्पृहणीयशील:क्षत्ता सकर्णानुजसौबलेन ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity ūcivāṁs tatra suyodhanenapravṛddha-kopa-sphuritādhareṇaasat-kṛtaḥ sat-spṛhaṇīya-śīlaḥkṣattā sakarṇānuja-saubalena
verses.text: While speaking thus, Vidura, whose personal character was esteemed by respectable persons, was insulted by Duryodhana, who was swollen with anger and whose lips were trembling. Duryodhana was in company with Karṇa, his younger brothers and his maternal uncle Śakuni.

verses.sanskrit: क एनमत्रोपजुहाव जिह्मंदास्या: सुतं यद्बलिनैव पुष्ट: ।तस्मिन् प्रतीप: परकृत्य आस्तेनिर्वास्यतामाशु पुराच्छ्‌वसान: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: ka enam atropajuhāva jihmaṁdāsyāḥ sutaṁ yad-balinaiva puṣṭaḥtasmin pratīpaḥ parakṛtya āstenirvāsyatām āśu purāc chvasānaḥ
verses.text: Who asked him to come here, this son of a kept mistress? He is so crooked that he spies in the interest of the enemy against those on whose support he has grown up. Toss him out of the palace immediately and leave him with only his breath.

verses.sanskrit: स्वयं धनुर्द्वारि निधाय मायांर्भ्रातु: पुरो मर्मसु ताडितोऽपि ।स इत्थमत्युल्बणकर्णबाणै-र्गतव्यथोऽयादुरु मानयान: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: svayaṁ dhanur dvāri nidhāya māyāṁbhrātuḥ puro marmasu tāḍito ’pisa ittham atyulbaṇa-karṇa-bāṇairgata-vyatho ’yād uru mānayānaḥ
verses.text: Thus being pierced by arrows through his ears and afflicted to the core of his heart, Vidura placed his bow on the door and quit his brother’s palace. He was not sorry, for he considered the acts of the external energy to be supreme.

verses.sanskrit: स निर्गत: कौरवपुण्यलब्धोगजाह्वयात्तीर्थपद: पदानि ।अन्वाक्रमत्पुण्यचिकीर्षयोर्व्यांअधिष्ठितो यानि सहस्रमूर्ति: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa nirgataḥ kaurava-puṇya-labdhogajāhvayāt tīrtha-padaḥ padānianvākramat puṇya-cikīrṣayorvyāmadhiṣṭhito yāni sahasra-mūrtiḥ
verses.text: By his piety, Vidura achieved the advantages of the pious Kauravas. After leaving Hastināpura, he took shelter of many places of pilgrimages, which are the Lord’s lotus feet. With a desire to gain a high order of pious life, he traveled to holy places where thousands of transcendental forms of the Lord are situated.

verses.sanskrit: पुरेषु पुण्योपवनाद्रिकुञ्जे-ष्वपङ्कतोयेषु सरित्सर:सु ।अनन्तलिङ्गै: समलङ्कृतेषुचचार तीर्थायतनेष्वनन्य: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: pureṣu puṇyopavanādri-kuñjeṣvapaṅka-toyeṣu sarit-saraḥsuananta-liṅgaiḥ samalaṅkṛteṣucacāra tīrthāyataneṣv ananyaḥ
verses.text: He began to travel alone, thinking only of Kṛṣṇa, through various holy places like Ayodhyā, Dvārakā and Mathurā. He traveled where the grove, hill, orchard, river and lake are all pure and sinless and where the forms of the Unlimited decorate the temples. Thus he performed the pilgrim’s progress.

verses.sanskrit: गां पर्यटन्मेध्यविविक्तवृत्ति:सदाप्लुतोऽध:शयनोऽवधूत: ।अलक्षित: स्वैरवधूतवेषोव्रतानि चेरे हरितोषणानि ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: gāṁ paryaṭan medhya-vivikta-vṛttiḥsadāpluto ’dhaḥ śayano ’vadhūtaḥalakṣitaḥ svair avadhūta-veṣovratāni cere hari-toṣaṇāni
verses.text: While so traversing the earth, he simply performed duties to please the Supreme Lord Hari. His occupation was pure and independent. He was constantly sanctified by taking his bath in holy places, although he was in the dress of a mendicant and had no hair dressing nor a bed on which to lie. Thus he was always unseen by his various relatives.

verses.sanskrit: इत्थं व्रजन् भारतमेव वर्षंकालेन यावद्‍गतवान् प्रभासम् ।तावच्छशास क्षितिमेकचक्रा-मेकातपत्रामजितेन पार्थ: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: itthaṁ vrajan bhāratam eva varṣaṁkālena yāvad gatavān prabhāsamtāvac chaśāsa kṣitim eka cakrāmekātapatrām ajitena pārthaḥ
verses.text: Thus, when he was in the land of Bhāratavarṣa traveling to all the places of pilgrimage, he visited Prabhāsakṣetra. At that time Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira was the emperor and held the world under one military strength and one flag.

verses.sanskrit: तत्राथ शुश्राव सुहृद्विनष्टिंवनं यथा वेणुजवह्निसंश्रयम् ।संस्पर्धया दग्धमथानुशोचन्सरस्वतीं प्रत्यगियाय तूष्णीम् ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatrātha śuśrāva suhṛd-vinaṣṭiṁvanaṁ yathā veṇuja-vahni-saṁśrayamsaṁspardhayā dagdham athānuśocansarasvatīṁ pratyag iyāya tūṣṇīm
verses.text: At the place of pilgrimage at Prabhāsa, it came to his knowledge that all his relatives had died due to violent passion, just as an entire forest burns due to fire produced by the friction of bamboos. After this he proceeded west, where the river Sarasvatī flows.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यां त्रितस्योशनसो मनोश्चपृथोरथाग्नेरसितस्य वायो: ।तीर्थं सुदासस्य गवां गुहस्ययच्छ्राद्धदेवस्य स आसिषेवे ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyāṁ tritasyośanaso manoś capṛthor athāgner asitasya vāyoḥtīrthaṁ sudāsasya gavāṁ guhasyayac chrāddhadevasya sa āsiṣeve
verses.text: On the bank of the river Sarasvatī there were eleven places of pilgrimage, namely (1) Trita, (2) Uśanā, (3) Manu, (4) Pṛthu, (5) Agni, (6) Asita, (7) Vāyu, (8) Sudāsa, (9) Go, (10) Guha and (11) Śrāddhadeva. Vidura visited all of them and duly performed rituals.

verses.sanskrit: अन्यानि चेह द्विजदेवदेवै:कृतानि नानायतनानि विष्णो: ।प्रत्यङ्गमुख्याङ्कितमन्दिराणियद्दर्शनात्कृष्णमनुस्मरन्ति ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: anyāni ceha dvija-deva-devaiḥkṛtāni nānāyatanāni viṣṇoḥpratyaṅga-mukhyāṅkita-mandirāṇiyad-darśanāt kṛṣṇam anusmaranti
verses.text: There were also many other temples of various forms of the Supreme Personality of Godhead Viṣṇu, established by great sages and demigods. These temples were marked with the chief emblems of the Lord, and they reminded one always of the original Personality of Godhead, Lord Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: ततस्त्वतिव्रज्य सुराष्ट्रमृद्धंसौवीरमत्स्यान् कुरुजाङ्गलांश्च ।कालेन तावद्यमुनामुपेत्यतत्रोद्धवं भागवतं ददर्श ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatas tv ativrajya surāṣṭram ṛddhaṁsauvīra-matsyān kurujāṅgalāṁś cakālena tāvad yamunām upetyatatroddhavaṁ bhāgavataṁ dadarśa
verses.text: Thereafter he passed through very wealthy provinces like Surat, Sauvīra and Matsya and through western India, known as Kurujāṅgala. At last he reached the bank of the Yamunā, where he happened to meet Uddhava, the great devotee of Lord Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: स वासुदेवानुचरं प्रशान्तंबृहस्पते: प्राक् तनयं प्रतीतम् ।आलिङ्ग्‍य गाढं प्रणयेन भद्रंस्वानामपृच्छद्भगवत्प्रजानाम् ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa vāsudevānucaraṁ praśāntaṁbṛhaspateḥ prāk tanayaṁ pratītamāliṅgya gāḍhaṁ praṇayena bhadraṁsvānām apṛcchad bhagavat-prajānām
verses.text: Then, due to his great love and feeling, Vidura embraced him [Uddhava], who was a constant companion of Lord Kṛṣṇa and formerly a great student of Bṛhaspati’s. Vidura then asked him for news of the family of Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: कच्चित्पुराणौ पुरुषौ स्वनाभ्य-पाद्मानुवृत्त्येह किलावतीर्णौ ।आसात उर्व्या: कुशलं विधायकृतक्षणौ कुशलं शूरगेहे ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaccit purāṇau puruṣau svanābhya-pādmānuvṛttyeha kilāvatīrṇauāsāta urvyāḥ kuśalaṁ vidhāyakṛta-kṣaṇau kuśalaṁ śūra-gehe
verses.text: [Please tell me] whether the original Personalities of Godhead, who incarnated Themselves at the request of Brahmā [who is born out of the lotus flower from the Lord] and who have increased the prosperity of the world by elevating everyone, are doing well in the house of Śūrasena.

verses.sanskrit: कच्चित्कुरूणां परम: सुहृन्नोभाम: स आस्ते सुखमङ्ग शौरि: ।यो वै स्वसृणां पितृवद्ददातिवरान् वदान्यो वरतर्पणेन ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaccit kurūṇāṁ paramaḥ suhṛn nobhāmaḥ sa āste sukham aṅga śauriḥyo vai svasṝṇāṁ pitṛvad dadātivarān vadānyo vara-tarpaṇena
verses.text: [Please tell me] whether the best friend of the Kurus, our brother-in-law Vasudeva, is doing well. He is very munificent. He is like a father to his sisters, and he is always pleasing to his wives.

verses.sanskrit: कच्चिद्वरूथाधिपतिर्यदूनांप्रद्युम्न आस्ते सुखमङ्ग वीर: ।यं रुक्‍मिणी भगवतोऽभिलेभेआराध्य विप्रान् स्मरमादिसर्गे ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaccid varūthādhipatir yadūnāṁpradyumna āste sukham aṅga vīraḥyaṁ rukmiṇī bhagavato ’bhilebheārādhya viprān smaram ādi-sarge
verses.text: O Uddhava, please tell me, How is Pradyumna, the commander in chief of the Yadus, who was Cupid in a former life? Rukmiṇī bore him as her son from Lord Kṛṣṇa, by the grace of brāhmaṇas whom she pleased.

verses.sanskrit: कच्चित्सुखं सात्वतवृष्णिभोज-दाशार्हकाणामधिप: स आस्ते ।यमभ्यषिञ्चच्छतपत्रनेत्रोनृपासनाशां परिहृत्य दूरात् ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaccit sukhaṁ sātvata-vṛṣṇi-bhoja-dāśārhakāṇām adhipaḥ sa āsteyam abhyaṣiñcac chata-patra-netronṛpāsanāśāṁ parihṛtya dūrāt
verses.text: O my friend, [tell me] whether Ugrasena, the King of the Sātvatas, Vṛṣṇis, Bhojas and Dāśārhas, is now doing well. He went far away from his kingdom, leaving aside all hopes of his royal throne, but Lord Kṛṣṇa again installed him.

verses.sanskrit: कच्चिद्धरे: सौम्य सुत: सद‍ृक्षआस्तेऽग्रणी रथिनां साधु साम्ब: ।असूत यं जाम्बवती व्रताढ्यादेवं गुहं योऽम्बिकया धृतोऽग्रे ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: kaccid dhareḥ saumya sutaḥ sadṛkṣaāste ’graṇī rathināṁ sādhu sāmbaḥasūta yaṁ jāmbavatī vratāḍhyādevaṁ guhaṁ yo ’mbikayā dhṛto ’gre
verses.text: O gentle one, does Sāmba fare well? He exactly resembles the son of the Personality of Godhead. In a previous birth he was born as Kārttikeya in the womb of the wife of Lord Śiva, and now he has been born in the womb of Jāmbavatī, the most enriched wife of Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: क्षेमं स कच्चिद्युयुधान आस्तेय: फाल्गुनाल्लब्धधनूरहस्य: ।लेभेऽञ्जसाधोक्षजसेवयैवगतिं तदीयां यतिभिर्दुरापाम् ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: kṣemaṁ sa kaccid yuyudhāna āsteyaḥ phālgunāl labdha-dhanū-rahasyaḥlebhe ’ñjasādhokṣaja-sevayaivagatiṁ tadīyāṁ yatibhir durāpām
verses.text: O Uddhava, does Yuyudhāna fare well? He learned the intricacies of the military art from Arjuna and attained the transcendental destination which is very difficult to reach even for great renouncers.

verses.sanskrit: कच्चिद् बुध: स्वस्त्यनमीव आस्तेश्वफल्कपुत्रो भगवत्प्रपन्न: ।य: कृष्णपादाङ्कितमार्गपांसु-ष्वचेष्टत प्रेमविभिन्नधैर्य: ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaccid budhaḥ svasty anamīva āsteśvaphalka-putro bhagavat-prapannaḥyaḥ kṛṣṇa-pādāṅkita-mārga-pāṁsuṣvaceṣṭata prema-vibhinna-dhairyaḥ
verses.text: Please tell me whether Akrūra, the son of Śvaphalka, is doing well. He is a faultless soul surrendered unto the Personality of Godhead. He once lost his mental equilibrium due to his ecstasy of transcendental love and fell down on the dust of a road which was marked with the footprints of Lord Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: कच्चिच्छिवं देवकभोजपुत्र्याविष्णुप्रजाया इव देवमातु: ।या वै स्वगर्भेण दधार देवंत्रयी यथा यज्ञवितानमर्थम् ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaccic chivaṁ devaka-bhoja-putryāviṣṇu-prajāyā iva deva-mātuḥyā vai sva-garbheṇa dadhāra devaṁtrayī yathā yajña-vitānam artham
verses.text: As the Vedas are the reservoir of sacrificial purposes, so the daughter of King Devaka-bhoja conceived the Supreme Personality of Godhead in her womb, as did the mother of the demigods. Is she [Devakī] doing well?

verses.sanskrit: अपिस्विदास्ते भगवान् सुखं वोय: सात्वतां कामदुघोऽनिरुद्ध: ।यमामनन्ति स्म हि शब्दयोनिंमनोमयं सत्त्वतुरीयतत्त्वम् ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: apisvid āste bhagavān sukhaṁ voyaḥ sātvatāṁ kāma-dugho ’niruddhaḥyam āmananti sma hi śabda-yoniṁmano-mayaṁ sattva-turīya-tattvam
verses.text: May I inquire whether Aniruddha is doing well? He is the fulfiller of all the desires of the pure devotees and has been considered from yore to be the cause of the Ṛg Veda, the creator of the mind and the fourth plenary expansion of Viṣṇu.

verses.sanskrit: अपिस्विदन्ये च निजात्मदैव-मनन्यवृत्त्या समनुव्रता ये ।हृदीकसत्यात्मजचारुदेष्ण-गदादय: स्वस्ति चरन्ति सौम्य ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: apisvid anye ca nijātma-daivamananya-vṛttyā samanuvratā yehṛdīka-satyātmaja-cārudeṣṇa-gadādayaḥ svasti caranti saumya
verses.text: O sober one, others, such as Hṛdīka, Cārudeṣṇa, Gada and the son of Satyabhāmā, who accept Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa as the soul of the self and thus follow His path without deviation — are they well?

verses.sanskrit: अपि स्वदोर्भ्यां विजयाच्युताभ्यांधर्मेण धर्म: परिपाति सेतुम् ।दुर्योधनोऽतप्यत यत्सभायांसाम्राज्यलक्ष्म्या विजयानुवृत्त्या ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: api sva-dorbhyāṁ vijayācyutābhyāṁdharmeṇa dharmaḥ paripāti setumduryodhano ’tapyata yat-sabhāyāṁsāmrājya-lakṣmyā vijayānuvṛttyā
verses.text: Also let me inquire whether Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira is now maintaining the kingdom according to religious principles and with respect for the path of religion. Formerly Duryodhana was burning with envy because Yudhiṣṭhira was being protected by the arms of Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna as if they were his own arms.

verses.sanskrit: किं वा कृताघेष्वघमत्यमर्षीभीमोऽहिवद्दीर्घतमं व्यमुञ्चत् ।यस्याङ्‌घ्रि पातं रणभूर्न सेहेमार्गं गदायाश्चरतो विचित्रम् ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: kiṁ vā kṛtāgheṣv agham atyamarṣībhīmo ’hivad dīrghatamaṁ vyamuñcatyasyāṅghri-pātaṁ raṇa-bhūr na sehemārgaṁ gadāyāś carato vicitram
verses.text: [Please tell me] whether the unconquerable Bhīma, who is like a cobra, has released his long-cherished anger upon the sinners? The field of battle could not tolerate even the wonderful playing of his club when he stepped on the path.

verses.sanskrit: कच्चिद्यशोधा रथयूथपानांगाण्डीवधन्वोपरतारिरास्ते ।अलक्षितो यच्छरकूटगूढोमायाकिरातो गिरिशस्तुतोष ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaccid yaśodhā ratha-yūthapānāṁgāṇḍīva-dhanvoparatārir āstealakṣito yac-chara-kūṭa-gūḍhomāyā-kirāto giriśas tutoṣa
verses.text: [Please tell me] whether Arjuna, whose bow bears the name Gāṇḍīva and who is always famous amongst the chariot warriors for vanquishing his enemies, is doing well. He once satisfied Lord Śiva by covering him with arrows when Śiva came as an unidentified false hunter.

verses.sanskrit: यमावुतस्वित्तनयौ पृथाया:पार्थैर्वृतौ पक्ष्मभिरक्षिणीव ।रेमात उद्दाय मृधे स्वरिक्थंपरात्सुपर्णाविव वज्रिवक्त्रात् ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: yamāv utasvit tanayau pṛthāyāḥpārthair vṛtau pakṣmabhir akṣiṇīvaremāta uddāya mṛdhe sva-rikthaṁparāt suparṇāv iva vajri-vaktrāt
verses.text: Are the twin brothers who are protected by their brothers doing well? Just as the eye is always protected by the eyelid, they are protected by the sons of Pṛthā, who snatched back their rightful kingdom from the hands of their enemy Duryodhana, just as Garuḍa snatched nectar from the mouth of Indra, the thunderbolt carrier.

verses.sanskrit: अहो पृथापि ध्रियतेऽर्भकार्थेराजर्षिवर्येण विनापि तेन ।यस्त्वेकवीरोऽधिरथो विजिग्येधनुर्द्वितीय: ककुभश्चतस्र: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: aho pṛthāpi dhriyate ’rbhakārtherājarṣi-varyeṇa vināpi tenayas tv eka-vīro ’dhiratho vijigyedhanur dvitīyaḥ kakubhaś catasraḥ
verses.text: O my lord, is Pṛthā still living? She lived only for the sake of her fatherless children; otherwise it was impossible for her to live without King Pāṇḍu, who was the greatest commander and who alone conquered the four directions simply with the help of a second bow.

verses.sanskrit: सौम्यानुशोचे तमध:पतन्तंभ्रात्रे परेताय विदुद्रुहे य: ।निर्यापितो येन सुहृत्स्वपुर्याअहं स्वपुत्रान् समनुव्रतेन ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: saumyānuśoce tam adhaḥ-patantaṁbhrātre paretāya vidudruhe yaḥniryāpito yena suhṛt sva-puryāahaṁ sva-putrān samanuvratena
verses.text: O gentle one, I simply lament for him [Dhṛtarāṣṭra] who rebelled against his brother after death. By him I was driven out of my own house, although I am his sincere well-wisher, because he accepted the line of action adopted by his own sons.

verses.sanskrit: सोऽहं हरेर्मर्त्यविडम्बनेनद‍ृशो नृणां चालयतो विधातु: ।नान्योपलक्ष्य: पदवीं प्रसादा-च्चरामि पश्यन् गतविस्मयोऽत्र ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: so ’haṁ harer martya-viḍambanenadṛśo nṛṇāṁ cālayato vidhātuḥnānyopalakṣyaḥ padavīṁ prasādāccarāmi paśyan gata-vismayo ’tra
verses.text: I am not astonished at this, having traveled over the world without being seen by others. The activities of the Personality of Godhead, which are like those of a man in this mortal world, are bewildering to others, but I know of His greatness by His grace, and thus I am happy in all respects.

verses.sanskrit: नूनं नृपाणां त्रिमदोत्पथानांमहीं मुहुश्चालयतां चमूभि: ।वधात्प्रपन्नार्तिजिहीर्षयेशो-ऽप्युपैक्षताघं भगवान् कुरूणाम् ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: nūnaṁ nṛpāṇāṁ tri-madotpathānāṁmahīṁ muhuś cālayatāṁ camūbhiḥvadhāt prapannārti-jihīrṣayeśo’py upaikṣatāghaṁ bhagavān kurūṇām
verses.text: Despite His being the Lord and being always willing to relieve the distress of sufferers, He [Kṛṣṇa] refrained from killing the Kurus, although they committed all sorts of sins and although He saw other kings constantly agitating the earth by their strong military movements carried out under the dictation of three kinds of false pride.

verses.sanskrit: अजस्य जन्मोत्पथनाशनायकर्माण्यकर्तुर्ग्रहणाय पुंसाम् ।नन्वन्यथा कोऽर्हति देहयोगंपरो गुणानामुत कर्मतन्त्रम् ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: ajasya janmotpatha-nāśanāyakarmāṇy akartur grahaṇāya puṁsāmnanv anyathā ko ’rhati deha-yogaṁparo guṇānām uta karma-tantram
verses.text: The appearance of the Lord is manifested for the annihilation of the upstarts. His activities are transcendental and are enacted for the understanding of all persons. Otherwise, since the Lord is transcendental to all material modes, what purpose could He serve by coming to earth?

verses.sanskrit: तस्य प्रपन्नाखिललोकपाना-मवस्थितानामनुशासने स्वे ।अर्थाय जातस्य यदुष्वजस्यवार्तां सखे कीर्तय तीर्थकीर्ते: ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya prapannākhila-lokapānāmavasthitānām anuśāsane svearthāya jātasya yaduṣv ajasyavārtāṁ sakhe kīrtaya tīrtha-kīrteḥ
verses.text: O my friend, please, therefore, chant the glories of the Lord, who is meant to be glorified in the places of pilgrimage. He is unborn, and yet He appears by His causeless mercy upon the surrendered rulers of all parts of the universe. Only for their interest did He appear in the family of His unalloyed devotees the Yadus.
